visuals.title: Storage Attachment Methods - DAS vs NAS vs SAN
visuals.description: Visual comparison of Direct Attached Storage, Network Attached Storage, and Storage Area Network architectures

diagrams.title: DAS vs NAS vs SAN Architecture
steps: DAS: Host → I/O Bus (SATA/SAS) → Disk (block-level, single host)
NAS: Clients → LAN (TCP/IP) → NAS Device → NFS/CIFS (file-level, shared)
SAN: Servers → SAN Fabric (FC/iSCSI) → Storage Arrays (block-level, shared)
DAS is cheapest and fastest but limited to one host
NAS is easy to share but limited by network bandwidth
SAN is high performance and shared but most expensive
diagrams.notes: Key distinction: NAS = file-level access, SAN = block-level access. DAS = direct connection, NAS/SAN = networked.
